report_title: Agent Provider System — TA Evaluation (4 Models, 4 Personas)
methodology: 4 different AI models, each assigned a distinct persona covering human + agent perspectives

evaluators.persona: Alex Chen — Solo Developer / Human Provider
evaluators.key_strength: Value proposition is compelling (10% vs RapidAPI 25%)
evaluators.key_weakness: Blockchain terminology alienates non-crypto developers; CTA buttons route to buyer docs instead of provider portal
Wallet funding requires human web UI; MCP descriptor is generic stub for all services

evaluators.persona: Sam Park — Startup CTO / Platform Builder
evaluators.key_strength: Business model is brilliant; Starter Kit is genuinely helpful
evaluators.key_weakness: SQLite is unscalable bottleneck; plaintext API key storage; no OpenAPI spec

evaluators.key_strength: Good security headers baseline; CORS restricted properly
evaluators.key_weakness: 9 critical vulnerabilities including IDOR, SSRF gaps, TOCTOU race conditions

critical: IDOR: Any authenticated user can read any provider's email/wallet/escrow — no ownership checks
SSRF: Service review pipeline doesn't block private IP ranges (10.x, 172.x, 169.254.x)
Daily transaction cap is non-atomic — race condition allows bypass under concurrency
Auto-delist weaponizable: 3 throwaway accounts can permanently delist any provider
Dynamic SQL column injection in update functions (dict keys interpolated into f-string SQL)
high: Wallet funding requires human web UI — agents cannot autonomously top up
MCP descriptor is generic stub — agents can't auto-generate valid API calls
SQLite single-writer architecture caps throughput at ~50-100 tx/sec
CTA buttons on providers page route to buyer documentation, not provider portal
Blockchain terminology (DID, wallet, USDC, Base L2) alienates non-crypto developers
medium: Provider registration endpoint (POST /services) not documented in API docs
No audit logging for escrow lifecycle events
Rate limiting is per-IP only, no per-API-key limits on financial endpoints
API responses leak PII (owner_email, wallet_address) to any authenticated caller
No SDK for JavaScript/Go — Python only
Escrow auto-release is cron-dependent, not triggered by blockchain confirmation

top_10_improvements_by_priority.issue: Fix IDOR across all Agent Provider endpoints
Block private IP ranges in service review SSRF protection

top_10_improvements_by_priority.effort: Small (single SQL UPDATE with WHERE clause)

top_10_improvements_by_priority.issue: Add anti-sybil measures to abuse reporting
top_10_improvements_by_priority.effort: Medium (require minimum transaction history to file reports)

top_10_improvements_by_priority.issue: Fix CTA routing — provider buttons should link to provider portal
Add ownership authorization to escrow endpoints
Enrich MCP descriptors with per-service schemas
top_10_improvements_by_priority.effort: Medium (require schema on registration, embed in descriptor)

top_10_improvements_by_priority.issue: Add crypto-for-beginners guide (wallet setup, USDC, DID explained)
Add audit logging for escrow and provider lifecycle events
Mask PII in API responses (hide email/wallet from non-owners)

usability_human.detail: Good value prop, bad onboarding for non-crypto users

usability_agent.detail: Clean API, but wallet funding and MCP stubs block autonomy

developer_experience.detail: SQLite bottleneck, no OpenAPI, sync-only SDK
